articles.title: US says it destroyed Iranian boats, Iran denies naval losses
articles.summary: US says it destroyed Iranian boats, Iran denies naval losses The US says its forces have destroyed six Iranian small boats and intercepted cruise missiles and drones during a new operation aimed at securing shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian officials rejected the US account. State television quoted a military official denying that any Iranian boats had been sunk, directly contradicting the US claims. US Admiral Brad Cooper, the head of Central Command, said on Monday that US forces carried out the action as part of efforts to keep the waterway open amid escalating tensions.

articles.title: South Korea reports ‘explosion and fire’ on ship in Hormuz, cause unclear
articles.summary: South Korea reports ‘explosion and fire’ on ship in Hormuz, cause unclear South Korea's foreign ministry reported on Monday that an “explosion and fire” struck a carrier owned by Korean shipper HMM in the Strait of Hormuz near the UAE while it was attempting to pass through. The ministry has reported “no casualties to date” and said that 24 crew members were on board the vessel, including 18 foreign nationals. A spokesperson for HMM told Reuters that it is not clear whether the fire was caused by an attack or happened internally, but the cause is being investigated.
